reasoning: The 2-year Treasury yield dropped sharply to 3.40% on Friday, Feb 13, 2026, marking a multi-year low and a steep decline from 3.52% just two days prior. The recent typical trading range has been 3.45%-3.57%. A drop this sudden suggests a strong likelihood of near-term mean reversion, bouncing back above 3.40%. The FRED historical analysis provides a combined 70% probability that the yield will be strictly above 3.40, which serves as a highly reliable anchor. I have slightly moderated this to 68% for the Feb 22 resolution to account for the clear dovish momentum and market expectations of Fed rate cuts in the near term pushing yields down. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, the estimate for March 17 is pulled closer to 0.5 (set at 58%), as the longer time horizon introduces more uncertainty over structural interest rate patterns and inflation metrics diverging from short-term noise.
